summary: A solid but occasionally muddy recording captures a soaring 1991 show highlighted by a blistering Slipknot and a stellar Help‑Slip‑Franklin sequence.
review: Fans rave about the high energy of this summer‑'91 performance, especially the focused Slipknot jam and the magical Help > Slip > Franklin’s stretch that launched the second set. The recording is generally good, though several listeners note muddy bass and distant instrument placement that let crowd noise intrude at times.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is praised across most reviews (5‑star comments) while recording quality receives mixed feedback (good to muddy). Weighted average yields a strong rating around 4.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear, but bass is described as muddy and distant; crowd cheering can drown the band in places.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set built good momentum; second set erupted with a powerful slip‑knot opening, flowing seamlessly into Help‑Slip‑Franklin and a soaring Dark Star, keeping the audience bouncing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Focused playing and great tone noted during Slipknot.
band_member_comments.Phil: Solid bass foundation despite some muddiness in the recording.
band_member_comments.Keys: Presence of Brent (referred to as Bobby) acknowledged; no specific performance comment.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums were on point, driving the energy throughout especially in the second set.
